acceptance_criteria: Backend deployment posture is expressed as wasmCloud on Wasmtime with deployable component metadata instead of native service binaries.
The OS shell subset is rehomed under ui/ and exposes only the governed release apps for this product profile.
SurrealDB access is centralized behind shared repository abstractions and UI data access stays on typed SDK/contracts.
Workspace tooling, CI, and process validation encode the new ui and wasmCloud-first rules.

open_questions.question: Nomad host/service topology can be refined further once real lattice rollout automation is introduced.

objective: Validate the integrated wasmCloud-first ui shell slice.
checks_run: cargo check -p sdk-rs -p surrealdb-access -p strategy-sandbox -p desktop_runtime -p desktop_app_contract -p desktop_app_control_center -p wasmcloud-bindings -p wasmcloud-smoke-tests -p treasury-disbursement -p finance-service -p quant-strategy-promotion
passed_checks: Targeted platform, service, workflow, and ui integration crates compile together.
Browser/Tauri shell packages compose with the rehomed ui workspace.
process-method enforces the new ui profile evidence requirements.

notes: Wasmtime-backed strategy loading and wasmCloud component metadata compile under the platform/runtime path.
WIT additions are versioned under schemas/wit/v1 without breaking existing Rust contracts.
Binding parity tests and smoke tests cover the new schema-to-runtime path.
Finance service component metadata references canonical service boundaries and schema refs.
Shared SurrealDB repositories keep data ownership out of ui/ and away from ad hoc callers.
Workflow adapters were added without bypassing workflow-owned orchestration paths.
Workflow runtime changes preserve compensation behavior inside workflow crates.
Policy-aware workflow/service interactions remain explicit in quant strategy promotion.
Workflow evidence and event outputs continue through typed contracts.
New repository access, task runner, and validation logic reuse shared crates rather than duplicating code.
Shared crates added no business-domain policy branching.
Host capabilities stay behind ui/platform_host and Tauri/web adapters.
Both browser preview and Tauri desktop crates compile in the updated workspace.
Control Center and runtime composition use sdk-rs platform snapshots instead of direct database clients.
Nomad control-plane manifests no longer model raw service binaries for backend workloads.
